title: Numerical Computing, Arrays, Visualization, and Reproducibility · Quantity Clinic: Floating Point
summary: Implement and test reusable computational models over real datasets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int floating point increment in a tested numerical Python notebook and module. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain floating-point approximation and compare values with justified tolerances.
Evaluate whose costs and benefits an objective includes or omits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Floating Point evidence
steps.instruction: Calculate the determinant of [[3, 1], [2, 6]]. Use the result to decide Floating Point for a tested numerical Python notebook and module.
steps.why: The Floating Point result for a tested numerical Python notebook and module is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (3 × 6) − (1 × 2). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain floating-point approximation and compare values with justified tolerances.
